human_verification.notes: Reviewed healthcare privacy boundary controls, HIPAA authorization and identity prerequisites, minimum-necessary handling, breach-sensitive evidence handling, legal/clinical/payer escalation rules, research-source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research source classes are represented.
All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for legal advice, final breach determination, unsupported disclosure approval, minimum-necessary waiver, identity-control waiver, clinical judgment, and payer interpretation.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers missing authorization or identity proofing, incomplete breach evidence, minimum-necessary conflict, normal disclosure intake, legal signoff refusal, low-confidence tenant gaps, policy conflicts, clinical escalation, payer dependencies, accounting evidence conflict, tenant adaptation, and workforce remediation.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build and approval event.
